revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称 = 三島神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=下田市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|40|35.2|N|138|57|40.8|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[下田市]]柿崎122 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|35.2|N|138|57|40.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[大国主命]] |社格 = [[式内社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 三島神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=下田市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|40|35.2|N|138|57|40.8|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[下田市]]柿崎122 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|35.2|N|138|57|40.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[大国主命]] |社格 = [[式内社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 柿崎神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=三島神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|40|35.2|N|138|57|40.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''三島神社'''（みしまじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[下田市]]柿崎（かきさき）にある[[神社]]<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 三島神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。地名を採って'''柿崎神社'''（かきさきじんじゃ）とも称される<ref>バス停「柿崎神社前」など</ref>。
== 概要 == 下田市の東方、柿崎(かきさき)地区の沿岸部、弁天島の向かいに鎮座している。
創建は不詳。
かつて同じ柿崎地区の西方、寝姿山の南端、武山(ぶざん)の南側中腹に、[[式内社]]である'''多祁富許都久和気命神社'''（たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ）、及び『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』の「従四位上 たつふたわけの明神」に比定されている古社である'''武山権現（武峯神社）'''が鎮座していたが、[[明治]]17年（[[1884年]]）に暴風により社殿が倒壊し、[[大正]]5年（[[1916年]]）に大火で焼失したことに伴い、三島神社に合祀された<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm 三島神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html 多祁富許都久和気命神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html 伝説「武山の役行者」] - 下田市</ref>。
柿崎の地は、江戸幕末に[[吉田松陰]]が密航を図った地として知られており、境内と向かいの弁天島には吉田松陰像が建てられている<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html 吉田松陰像（下田）] - 伊豆・駿河観光ガイド</ref>。
== 祭神 == * [[大国主命]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 三島神社] - 静岡県神社庁
{{デフォルトソート:みしましんしや しもたし}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:下田市の建築物]] [[Category:下田市の歴史]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:三島・大山祇信仰]]

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine (Shimoda)'''|三島神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine (Shimoda)'''|三島神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine (Shimoda)'''|三島神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine (Shimoda)'''|三島神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine (Shimoda)'''|三島神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine (Shimoda)'''|三島神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine (Shimoda)'''|三島神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

revision.comment: 1 revision imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 三島神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=下田市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|40|35.2|N|138|57|40.8|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[下田市]]柿崎122 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|35.2|N|138|57|40.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[大国主命]] |社格 = [[式内社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 柿崎神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=三島神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|40|35.2|N|138|57|40.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''三島神社'''（みしまじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[下田市]]柿崎（かきさき）にある[[神社]]<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 三島神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。地名を採って'''柿崎神社'''（かきさきじんじゃ）とも称される<ref>バス停「柿崎神社前」など</ref>。
== 概要 == 下田市の東方、柿崎(かきさき)地区の沿岸部、弁天島の向かいに鎮座している。
創建は不詳。
かつて同じ柿崎地区の西方、寝姿山の南端、武山(ぶざん)の南側中腹に、[[式内社]]である'''多祁富許都久和気命神社'''（たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ）、及び『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』の「従四位上 たつふたわけの明神」に比定されている古社である'''武山権現（武峯神社）'''が鎮座していたが、[[明治]]17年（[[1884年]]）に暴風により社殿が倒壊し、[[大正]]5年（[[1916年]]）に大火で焼失したことに伴い、三島神社に合祀された<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm 三島神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html 多祁富許都久和気命神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html 伝説「武山の役行者」] - 下田市</ref>。
柿崎の地は、江戸幕末に[[吉田松陰]]が密航を図った地として知られており、境内と向かいの弁天島には吉田松陰像が建てられている<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html 吉田松陰像（下田）] - 伊豆・駿河観光ガイド</ref>。
== 祭神 == * [[大国主命]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 三島神社] - 静岡県神社庁

かつて同じ柿崎地区の西方、寝姿山の南端、武山(ぶざん)の南側中腹に、[[式内社]]である'''多祁富許都久和気命神社'''（たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ）、及び『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』の「従四位上 たつふたわけの明神」に比定されている古社である'''武山権現（武峯神社）'''が鎮座していたが、[[明治]]17年（[[1884年]]）に暴風により社殿が倒壊し、[[大正]]5年（[[1916年]]）に大火で焼失したことに伴い、三島神社に合祀された<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm 三島神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html 多祁富許都久和気命神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html 伝説「武山の役行者」] - 下田市</ref>。
柿崎の地は、江戸幕末に[[吉田松陰]]が密航を図った地として知られており、境内と向かいの弁天島には吉田松陰像が建てられている<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html 吉田松陰像（下田）] - 伊豆・駿河観光ガイド</ref>。
== 祭神 == * [[大国主命]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 三島神社] - 静岡県神社庁

[[JA:三島神社 (下田市)]]
{{wikidata link|Q134930744}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
{{translated page|ja|三島神社 (下田市)|version=103511941|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 三島神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=下田市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|40|35.2|N|138|57|40.8|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|下田市|Q653402}}柿崎122 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|35.2|N|138|57|40.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|大国主命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 柿崎神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=三島神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|40|35.2|N|138|57|40.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''三島神社'''（みしまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|下田市|Q653402}}柿崎（かきさき）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 三島神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。地名を採って'''柿崎神社'''（かきさきじんじゃ）とも称される<ref>バス停「柿崎神社前」など</ref>。
== 概要 == 下田市の東方、柿崎(かきさき)地区の沿岸部、弁天島の向かいに鎮座している。
創建は不詳。
かつて同じ柿崎地区の西方、寝姿山の南端、武山(ぶざん)の南側中腹に、{{jalink|式内社}}である'''多祁富許都久和気命神社'''（たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ）、及び『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』の「従四位上 たつふたわけの明神」に比定されている古社である'''武山権現（武峯神社）'''が鎮座していたが、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}17年（{{jalink|1884年|Q7819}}）に暴風により社殿が倒壊し、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}5年（{{jalink|1916年|Q2087}}）に大火で焼失したことに伴い、三島神社に合祀された<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm 三島神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html 多祁富許都久和気命神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html 伝説「武山の役行者」] - 下田市</ref>。
柿崎の地は、江戸幕末に{{jalink|吉田松陰|Q559747}}が密航を図った地として知られており、境内と向かいの弁天島には吉田松陰像が建てられている<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html 吉田松陰像（下田）] - 伊豆・駿河観光ガイド</ref>。
== 祭神 == * {{jalink|大国主命}}

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine (Shimoda)'''|三島神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''三島神社'''（みしまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|下田市|Q653402}}柿崎（かきさき）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 三島神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。地名を採って'''柿崎神社'''（かきさきじんじゃ）とも称される<ref>バス停「柿崎神社前」など</ref>。

=== 概要 ===
下田市の東方、柿崎(かきさき)地区の沿岸部、弁天島の向かいに鎮座している。

創建は不詳。

かつて同じ柿崎地区の西方、寝姿山の南端、武山(ぶざん)の南側中腹に、{{jalink|式内社}}である'''多祁富許都久和気命神社'''（たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ）、及び『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』の「従四位上 たつふたわけの明神」に比定されている古社である'''武山権現（武峯神社）'''が鎮座していたが、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}17年（{{jalink|1884年|Q7819}}）に暴風により社殿が倒壊し、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}5年（{{jalink|1916年|Q2087}}）に大火で焼失したことに伴い、三島神社に合祀された<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm 三島神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html 多祁富許都久和気命神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html 伝説「武山の役行者」] - 下田市</ref>。

柿崎の地は、江戸幕末に{{jalink|吉田松陰|Q559747}}が密航を図った地として知られており、境内と向かいの弁天島には吉田松陰像が建てられている<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html 吉田松陰像（下田）] - 伊豆・駿河観光ガイド</ref>。

[[JA:三島神社 (下田市)]]

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称 = 三島神社
|画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=下田市}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|40|35.2|N|138|57|40.8|E}}}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|下田市|Q653402}}柿崎122
|位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|35.2|N|138|57|40.8|E|display=inline,title}}

{{coord2|34|40|35.2|N|138|57|40.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
}}
'''三島神社'''（みしまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|下田市|Q653402}}柿崎（かきさき）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 三島神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。地名を採って'''柿崎神社'''（かきさきじんじゃ）とも称される<ref>バス停「柿崎神社前」など</ref>。

=== 概要 ===
下田市の東方、柿崎(かきさき)地区の沿岸部、弁天島の向かいに鎮座している。

創建は不詳。

かつて同じ柿崎地区の西方、寝姿山の南端、武山(ぶざん)の南側中腹に、{{jalink|式内社}}である'''多祁富許都久和気命神社'''（たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ）、及び『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』の「従四位上 たつふたわけの明神」に比定されている古社である'''武山権現（武峯神社）'''が鎮座していたが、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}17年（{{jalink|1884年|Q7819}}）に暴風により社殿が倒壊し、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}5年（{{jalink|1916年|Q2087}}）に大火で焼失したことに伴い、三島神社に合祀された<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm 三島神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html 多祁富許都久和気命神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html 伝説「武山の役行者」] - 下田市</ref>。

柿崎の地は、江戸幕末に{{jalink|吉田松陰|Q559747}}が密航を図った地として知られており、境内と向かいの弁天島には吉田松陰像が建てられている<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html 吉田松陰像（下田）] - 伊豆・駿河観光ガイド</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* {{jalink|大国主命}}

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine (Shimoda)'''|三島神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

[[JA:三島神社 (下田市)]]
{{wikidata link|Q134930744}}
== Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Mishima Shrine |Image = {{Image needed|cat=Shimoda City}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|40|35.2|N|138|57|40.8|E}}}} |Location = Kakizaki 122, Shimoda City, Shizuoka Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates DMS|34|40|35.2|N|138|57|40.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto |Shrine rank = Shikinaisha |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = Kakizaki Shrine |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = October 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Mishima Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|40|35.2|N|138|57|40.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Mishima Shrine''' (みしまじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakizaki, Shimoda City, Shizuoka Prefecture.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 Mishima Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> It is also known as '''Kakizaki Shrine''' (かきさきじんじゃ), named after the place.<ref>e.g., the bus stop “Kakizaki Shrine-mae”</ref>
=== Overview === The shrine is enshrined along the coast of the Kakizaki district, east of Shimoda City, facing Bentenjima Island.
The date of its founding is unknown.
In the past, on the western side of the same Kakizaki district—on the southern end of Mount Nesugata, midway up the southern slope of Mount Buzan—there stood the ancient shrine '''Takehokotsukuwake-no-Mikoto Shrine''', a Shikinaisha, as well as '''Buzan Gongen (Buhō Shrine)''', an old shrine identified with the “Junior Fourth Rank Upper Tatsufutawake Myōjin” listed in the *Izu Province Shrine Rank Register*. However, after the shrine buildings collapsed in a violent storm in Meiji 17 (1884) and were later destroyed by a major fire in Taishō 5 (1916), these shrines were amalgamated into Mishima Shrine.<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm Mishima Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html Takehokotsukuwake-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html Legend: “En no Gyōja of Mount Buzan”] - Shimoda City</ref>
Kakizaki is also known as the place where Yoshida Shōin attempted to stow away on a ship at the end of the Edo period, and statues of Yoshida Shōin have been erected within the shrine grounds and on the opposite Bentenjima Island.<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html Yoshida Shōin Statue (Shimoda)] - Izu–Suruga Tourism Guide</ref>
=== Enshrined Deity === * Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine (Shimoda)'''|三島神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称 = 三島神社
|画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=下田市}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|40|35.2|N|138|57|40.8|E}}}}

{{coord2|34|40|35.2|N|138|57|40.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
}}
'''三島神社'''（みしまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|下田市|Q653402}}柿崎（かきさき）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 三島神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。地名を採って'''柿崎神社'''（かきさきじんじゃ）とも称される<ref>バス停「柿崎神社前」など</ref>。

=== 概要 ===
下田市の東方、柿崎(かきさき)地区の沿岸部、弁天島の向かいに鎮座している。

創建は不詳。

かつて同じ柿崎地区の西方、寝姿山の南端、武山(ぶざん)の南側中腹に、{{jalink|式内社}}である'''多祁富許都久和気命神社'''（たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ）、及び『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』の「従四位上 たつふたわけの明神」に比定されている古社である'''武山権現（武峯神社）'''が鎮座していたが、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}17年（{{jalink|1884年|Q7819}}）に暴風により社殿が倒壊し、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}5年（{{jalink|1916年|Q2087}}）に大火で焼失したことに伴い、三島神社に合祀された<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm 三島神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html 多祁富許都久和気命神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html 伝説「武山の役行者」] - 下田市</ref>。

柿崎の地は、江戸幕末に{{jalink|吉田松陰|Q559747}}が密航を図った地として知られており、境内と向かいの弁天島には吉田松陰像が建てられている<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html 吉田松陰像（下田）] - 伊豆・駿河観光ガイド</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine (Shimoda)'''|三島神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''三島神社'''（みしまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|下田市|Q653402}}柿崎（かきさき）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 三島神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。地名を採って'''柿崎神社'''（かきさきじんじゃ）とも称される<ref>バス停「柿崎神社前」など</ref>。

=== 概要 ===
下田市の東方、柿崎(かきさき)地区の沿岸部、弁天島の向かいに鎮座している。

創建は不詳。

かつて同じ柿崎地区の西方、寝姿山の南端、武山(ぶざん)の南側中腹に、{{jalink|式内社}}である'''多祁富許都久和気命神社'''（たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ）、及び『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』の「従四位上 たつふたわけの明神」に比定されている古社である'''武山権現（武峯神社）'''が鎮座していたが、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}17年（{{jalink|1884年|Q7819}}）に暴風により社殿が倒壊し、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}5年（{{jalink|1916年|Q2087}}）に大火で焼失したことに伴い、三島神社に合祀された<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm 三島神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html 多祁富許都久和気命神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html 伝説「武山の役行者」] - 下田市</ref>。

柿崎の地は、江戸幕末に{{jalink|吉田松陰|Q559747}}が密航を図った地として知られており、境内と向かいの弁天島には吉田松陰像が建てられている<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html 吉田松陰像（下田）] - 伊豆・駿河観光ガイド</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* {{jalink|大国主命}}

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine (Shimoda)'''|三島神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Mishima Shrine''' (みしまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Kakizaki, {{jalink|Shimoda City|Q653402}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 Mishima Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> Taking its name from the place-name, it is also called '''Kakizaki Shrine''' (かきさきじんじゃ).<ref>For example, the bus stop “Kakizaki Shrine-mae,” etc.</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined on the coastal area of the Kakizaki district, east of Shimoda City, opposite Benten Island.

The date of its founding is unknown.

In the past, on the western side of the same Kakizaki district—on the southern end of Nesugata-yama, on the southern mid-slope of Buzan—there stood the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} '''Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine''' (たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ), as well as the ancient shrine '''Buzan Gongen (Takemine Shrine)''', which is identified with the deity “Jushii-jō Tatsufutawake no Myōjin” listed in the '{{jalink|Izu Province Shinkaichō|Q17193087}}'. However, after the shrine buildings collapsed due to a violent storm in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 17 ({{jalink|1884|Q7819}}), and were subsequently destroyed by a great fire in {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 5 ({{jalink|1916|Q2087}}), these shrines were merged and enshrined together at Mishima Shrine.<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm Mishima Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html Legend: “En no Gyōja of Mount Buzan”] - Shimoda City</ref>

The Kakizaki area is known as the place where {{jalink|Yoshida Shōin|Q559747}} attempted to stow away at the end of the Edo period, and statues of Yoshida Shōin have been erected within the shrine grounds and on the opposite Benten Island.<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html Statue of Yoshida Shōin (Shimoda)] - Izu & Suruga Tourism Guide</ref>

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{jalink|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}

'''Mishima Shrine''' (みしまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Kakizaki, {{jalink|Shimoda City|Q653402}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 Mishima Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> Taking its name from the place-name, it is also called '''Kakizaki Shrine''' (かきさきじんじゃ).<ref>For example, the bus stop “Kakizaki Shrine-mae,” etc.</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined on the coastal area of the Kakizaki district, east of Shimoda City, opposite Benten Island.

The date of its founding is unknown.

In the past, on the western side of the same Kakizaki district—on the southern end of Nesugata-yama, on the southern mid-slope of Buzan—there stood the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} '''Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine''' (たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ), as well as the ancient shrine '''Buzan Gongen (Takemine Shrine)''', which is identified with the deity “Jushii-jō Tatsufutawake no Myōjin” listed in the '{{jalink|Izu Province Shinkaichō|Q17193087}}'. However, after the shrine buildings collapsed due to a violent storm in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 17 ({{jalink|1884|Q7819}}), and were subsequently destroyed by a great fire in {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 5 ({{jalink|1916|Q2087}}), these shrines were merged and enshrined together at Mishima Shrine.<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm Mishima Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html Legend: “En no Gyōja of Mount Buzan”] - Shimoda City</ref>

The Kakizaki area is known as the place where {{jalink|Yoshida Shōin|Q559747}} attempted to stow away at the end of the Edo period, and statues of Yoshida Shōin have been erected within the shrine grounds and on the opposite Benten Island.<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html Statue of Yoshida Shōin (Shimoda)] - Izu & Suruga Tourism Guide</ref>

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{jalink|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine (Shimoda)'''|三島神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Mishima Shrine''' (みしまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Kakizaki, {{jalink|Shimoda City|Q653402}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 Mishima Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> Taking its name from the place-name, it is also called '''Kakizaki Shrine''' (かきさきじんじゃ).<ref>For example, the bus stop “Kakizaki Shrine-mae,” etc.</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined on the coastal area of the Kakizaki district, east of Shimoda City, opposite Benten Island.

The date of its founding is unknown.

In the past, on the western side of the same Kakizaki district—on the southern end of Nesugata-yama, on the southern mid-slope of Buzan—there stood the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} '''Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine''' (たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ), as well as the ancient shrine '''Buzan Gongen (Takemine Shrine)''', which is identified with the deity “Jushii-jō Tatsufutawake no Myōjin” listed in the '{{jalink|Izu Province Shinkaichō|Q17193087}}'. However, after the shrine buildings collapsed due to a violent storm in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 17 ({{jalink|1884|Q7819}}), and were subsequently destroyed by a great fire in {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 5 ({{jalink|1916|Q2087}}), these shrines were merged and enshrined together at Mishima Shrine.<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm Mishima Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html Legend: “En no Gyōja of Mount Buzan”] - Shimoda City</ref>

The Kakizaki area is known as the place where {{jalink|Yoshida Shōin|Q559747}} attempted to stow away at the end of the Edo period, and statues of Yoshida Shōin have been erected within the shrine grounds and on the opposite Benten Island.<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html Statue of Yoshida Shōin (Shimoda)] - Izu & Suruga Tourism Guide</ref>

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{jalink|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine (Shimoda)'''|三島神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{Shrine

'''Mishima Shrine''' (みしまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Kakizaki, {{jalink|Shimoda City|Q653402}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 Mishima Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> Taking its name from the place-name, it is also called '''Kakizaki Shrine''' (かきさきじんじゃ).<ref>For example, the bus stop “Kakizaki Shrine-mae,” etc.</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined on the coastal area of the Kakizaki district, east of Shimoda City, opposite Benten Island.

The date of its founding is unknown.

In the past, on the western side of the same Kakizaki district—on the southern end of Nesugata-yama, on the southern mid-slope of Buzan—there stood the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} '''Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine''' (たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ), as well as the ancient shrine '''Buzan Gongen (Takemine Shrine)''', which is identified with the deity “Jushii-jō Tatsufutawake no Myōjin” listed in the '{{jalink|Izu Province Shinkaichō|Q17193087}}'. However, after the shrine buildings collapsed due to a violent storm in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 17 ({{jalink|1884|Q7819}}), and were subsequently destroyed by a great fire in {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 5 ({{jalink|1916|Q2087}}), these shrines were merged and enshrined together at Mishima Shrine.<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm Mishima Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html Legend: “En no Gyōja of Mount Buzan”] - Shimoda City</ref>

The Kakizaki area is known as the place where {{jalink|Yoshida Shōin|Q559747}} attempted to stow away at the end of the Edo period, and statues of Yoshida Shōin have been erected within the shrine grounds and on the opposite Benten Island.<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html Statue of Yoshida Shōin (Shimoda)] - Izu & Suruga Tourism Guide</ref>

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{jalink|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine (Shimoda)'''|三島神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Mishima Shrine''' (みしまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Kakizaki, {{jalink|Shimoda City|Q653402}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 Mishima Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> Taking its name from the place-name, it is also called '''Kakizaki Shrine''' (かきさきじんじゃ).<ref>For example, the bus stop “Kakizaki Shrine-mae,” etc.</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined on the coastal area of the Kakizaki district, east of Shimoda City, opposite Benten Island.

The date of its founding is unknown.

In the past, on the western side of the same Kakizaki district—on the southern end of Nesugata-yama, on the southern mid-slope of Buzan—there stood the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} '''Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine''' (たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ), as well as the ancient shrine '''Buzan Gongen (Takemine Shrine)''', which is identified with the deity “Jushii-jō Tatsufutawake no Myōjin” listed in the '{{jalink|Izu Province Shinkaichō|Q17193087}}'. However, after the shrine buildings collapsed due to a violent storm in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 17 ({{jalink|1884|Q7819}}), and were subsequently destroyed by a great fire in {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 5 ({{jalink|1916|Q2087}}), these shrines were merged and enshrined together at Mishima Shrine.<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm Mishima Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html Legend: “En no Gyōja of Mount Buzan”] - Shimoda City</ref>

The Kakizaki area is known as the place where {{jalink|Yoshida Shōin|Q559747}} attempted to stow away at the end of the Edo period, and statues of Yoshida Shōin have been erected within the shrine grounds and on the opposite Benten Island.<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html Statue of Yoshida Shōin (Shimoda)] - Izu & Suruga Tourism Guide</ref>

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{jalink|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 Mishima Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined on the coastal area of the Kakizaki district, east of Shimoda City, opposite Benten Island.

The date of its founding is unknown.

In the past, on the western side of the same Kakizaki district—on the southern end of Nesugata-yama, on the southern mid-slope of Buzan—there stood the {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} '''Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine''' (たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ), as well as the ancient shrine '''Buzan Gongen (Takemine Shrine)''', which is identified with the deity “Jushii-jō Tatsufutawake no Myōjin” listed in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}}'. {{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine (Shimoda)'''|三島神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Mishima Shrine''' (みしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Kakizaki, {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 Mishima Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> Taking its name from the place-name, it is also called '''Kakizaki Shrine''' (かきさきじんじゃ).<ref>For example, the bus stop “Kakizaki Shrine-mae,” etc.</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined on the coastal area of the Kakizaki district, east of Shimoda City, opposite Benten Island.

The date of its founding is unknown.

In the past, on the western side of the same Kakizaki district—on the southern end of Nesugata-yama, on the southern mid-slope of Buzan—there stood the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} '''Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine''' (たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ), as well as the ancient shrine '''Buzan Gongen (Takemine Shrine)''', which is identified with the deity “Jushii-jō Tatsufutawake no Myōjin” listed in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}}'. However, after the shrine buildings collapsed due to a violent storm in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819}}), and were subsequently destroyed by a great fire in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087}}), these shrines were merged and enshrined together at Mishima Shrine.<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm Mishima Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html Legend: “En no Gyōja of Mount Buzan”] - Shimoda City</ref>

The Kakizaki area is known as the place where {{ill|lt=Yoshida Shōin|Q559747|WD=Q559747}} attempted to stow away at the end of the Edo period, and statues of Yoshida Shōin have been erected within the shrine grounds and on the opposite Benten Island.<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html Statue of Yoshida Shōin (Shimoda)] - Izu & Suruga Tourism Guide</ref>

'''Mishima Shrine''' (みしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Kakizaki, {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 Mishima Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> Taking its name from the place-name, it is also called '''Kakizaki Shrine''' (かきさきじんじゃ).<ref>For example, the bus stop “Kakizaki Shrine-mae,” etc.</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined on the coastal area of the Kakizaki district, east of Shimoda City, opposite Benten Island.

The date of its founding is unknown.

In the past, on the western side of the same Kakizaki district—on the southern end of Nesugata-yama, on the southern mid-slope of Buzan—there stood the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} '''Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine''' (たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ), as well as the ancient shrine '''Buzan Gongen (Takemine Shrine)''', which is identified with the deity “Jushii-jō Tatsufutawake no Myōjin” listed in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}}'. However, after the shrine buildings collapsed due to a violent storm in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819}}), and were subsequently destroyed by a great fire in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087}}), these shrines were merged and enshrined together at Mishima Shrine.<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm Mishima Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html Legend: “En no Gyōja of Mount Buzan”] - Shimoda City</ref>

The Kakizaki area is known as the place where {{ill|lt=Yoshida Shōin|Q559747|WD=Q559747}} attempted to stow away at the end of the Edo period, and statues of Yoshida Shōin have been erected within the shrine grounds and on the opposite Benten Island.<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html Statue of Yoshida Shōin (Shimoda)] - Izu & Suruga Tourism Guide</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine (Shimoda)'''|三島神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Mishima Shrine''' (みしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Kakizaki, {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 Mishima Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> Taking its name from the place-name, it is also called '''Kakizaki Shrine''' (かきさきじんじゃ).<ref>For example, the bus stop “Kakizaki Shrine-mae,” etc.</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined on the coastal area of the Kakizaki district, east of Shimoda City, opposite Benten Island.

The date of its founding is unknown.

In the past, on the western side of the same Kakizaki district—on the southern end of Nesugata-yama, on the southern mid-slope of Buzan—there stood the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} '''Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine''' (たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ), as well as the ancient shrine '''Buzan Gongen (Takemine Shrine)''', which is identified with the deity “Jushii-jō Tatsufutawake no Myōjin” listed in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}}'. However, after the shrine buildings collapsed due to a violent storm in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819}}), and were subsequently destroyed by a great fire in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087}}), these shrines were merged and enshrined together at Mishima Shrine.<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm Mishima Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html Legend: “En no Gyōja of Mount Buzan”] - Shimoda City</ref>

The Kakizaki area is known as the place where {{ill|lt=Yoshida Shōin|Q559747|WD=Q559747}} attempted to stow away at the end of the Edo period, and statues of Yoshida Shōin have been erected within the shrine grounds and on the opposite Benten Island.<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html Statue of Yoshida Shōin (Shimoda)] - Izu & Suruga Tourism Guide</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine (Shimoda)'''|三島神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Mishima Shrine''' (みしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Kakizaki, {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 Mishima Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> Taking its name from the place-name, it is also called '''Kakizaki Shrine''' (かきさきじんじゃ).<ref>For example, the bus stop “Kakizaki Shrine-mae,” etc.</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined on the coastal area of the Kakizaki district, east of Shimoda City, opposite Benten Island.

The date of its founding is unknown.

In the past, on the western side of the same Kakizaki district—on the southern end of Nesugata-yama, on the southern mid-slope of Buzan—there stood the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} '''Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine''' (たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ), as well as the ancient shrine '''Buzan Gongen (Takemine Shrine)''', which is identified with the deity “Jushii-jō Tatsufutawake no Myōjin” listed in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}}'. However, after the shrine buildings collapsed due to a violent storm in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819}}), and were subsequently destroyed by a great fire in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087}}), these shrines were merged and enshrined together at Mishima Shrine.<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm Mishima Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html Legend: “En no Gyōja of Mount Buzan”] - Shimoda City</ref>

The Kakizaki area is known as the place where {{ill|lt=Yoshida Shōin|Q559747|WD=Q559747}} attempted to stow away at the end of the Edo period, and statues of Yoshida Shōin have been erected within the shrine grounds and on the opposite Benten Island.<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html Statue of Yoshida Shōin (Shimoda)] - Izu & Suruga Tourism Guide</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine (Shimoda)'''|三島神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{Shrine
|Name = Mishima Shrine
|Image = {{Image wanted|cat=Shimoda City}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|40|35.2|N|138|57|40.8|E}}}}

|Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q276944}}

{{coord2|34|40|35.2|N|138|57|40.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}

'''Mishima Shrine''' (みしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Kakizaki, {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 Mishima Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> Taking its name from the place-name, it is also called '''Kakizaki Shrine''' (かきさきじんじゃ).<ref>For example, the bus stop “Kakizaki Shrine-mae,” etc.</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined on the coastal area of the Kakizaki district, east of Shimoda City, opposite Benten Island.

The date of its founding is unknown.

In the past, on the western side of the same Kakizaki district—on the southern end of Nesugata-yama, on the southern mid-slope of Buzan—there stood the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} '''Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine''' (たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ), as well as the ancient shrine '''Buzan Gongen (Takemine Shrine)''', which is identified with the deity “Jushii-jō Tatsufutawake no Myōjin” listed in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}}'. However, after the shrine buildings collapsed due to a violent storm in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819}}), and were subsequently destroyed by a great fire in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087}}), these shrines were merged and enshrined together at Mishima Shrine.<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm Mishima Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html Legend: “En no Gyōja of Mount Buzan”] - Shimoda City</ref>

The Kakizaki area is known as the place where {{ill|lt=Yoshida Shōin|Q559747|WD=Q559747}} attempted to stow away at the end of the Edo period, and statues of Yoshida Shōin have been erected within the shrine grounds and on the opposite Benten Island.<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html Statue of Yoshida Shōin (Shimoda)] - Izu & Suruga Tourism Guide</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine (Shimoda)'''|三島神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Mishima Shrine''' (みしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kakizaki, {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402|1=Shimoda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 Mishima Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> Taking its name from the place-name, it is also called '''Kakizaki Shrine''' (かきさきじんじゃ).<ref>For example, the bus stop “Kakizaki Shrine-mae,” etc.</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined on the coastal area of the Kakizaki district, east of Shimoda City, opposite Benten Island.

The date of its founding is unknown.

In the past, on the western side of the same Kakizaki district—on the southern end of Nesugata-yama, on the southern mid-slope of Buzan—there stood the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} '''Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine''' (たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ), as well as the ancient shrine '''Buzan Gongen (Takemine Shrine)''', which is identified with the deity “Jushii-jō Tatsufutawake no Myōjin” listed in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu Province Shinkaichō}}'. However, after the shrine buildings collapsed due to a violent storm in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819|1=1884}}), and were subsequently destroyed by a great fire in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087|1=1916}}), these shrines were merged and enshrined together at Mishima Shrine.<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm Mishima Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html Legend: “En no Gyōja of Mount Buzan”] - Shimoda City</ref>

The Kakizaki area is known as the place where {{ill|lt=Yoshida Shōin|Q559747|WD=Q559747|1=Yoshida Shōin}} attempted to stow away at the end of the Edo period, and statues of Yoshida Shōin have been erected within the shrine grounds and on the opposite Benten Island.<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html Statue of Yoshida Shōin (Shimoda)] - Izu & Suruga Tourism Guide</ref>

The date of its founding is unknown.

In the past, on the western side of the same Kakizaki district—on the southern end of Nesugata-yama, on the southern mid-slope of Buzan—there stood the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} '''Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine''' (たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ), as well as the ancient shrine '''Buzan Gongen (Takemine Shrine)''', which is identified with the deity “Jushii-jō Tatsufutawake no Myōjin” listed in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu Province Shinkaichō}}'. However, after the shrine buildings collapsed due to a violent storm in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819|1=1884}}), and were subsequently destroyed by a great fire in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087|1=1916}}), these shrines were merged and enshrined together at Mishima Shrine.<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm Mishima Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html Legend: “En no Gyōja of Mount Buzan”] - Shimoda City</ref>

The Kakizaki area is known as the place where {{ill|lt=Yoshida Shōin|Q559747|WD=Q559747|1=Yoshida Shōin}} attempted to stow away at the end of the Edo period, and statues of Yoshida Shōin have been erected within the shrine grounds and on the opposite Benten Island.<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html Statue of Yoshida Shōin (Shimoda)] - Izu & Suruga Tourism Guide</ref>

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}

=== Notes ===

'''Mishima Shrine''' (みしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kakizaki, {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402|1=Shimoda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 Mishima Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> Taking its name from the place-name, it is also called '''Kakizaki Shrine''' (かきさきじんじゃ).<ref>For example, the bus stop “Kakizaki Shrine-mae,” etc.</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined on the coastal area of the Kakizaki district, east of Shimoda City, opposite Benten Island.

The date of its founding is unknown.

In the past, on the western side of the same Kakizaki district—on the southern end of Nesugata-yama, on the southern mid-slope of Buzan—there stood the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} '''Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine''' (たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ), as well as the ancient shrine '''Buzan Gongen (Takemine Shrine)''', which is identified with the deity “Jushii-jō Tatsufutawake no Myōjin” listed in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu Province Shinkaichō}}'. However, after the shrine buildings collapsed due to a violent storm in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819|1=1884}}), and were subsequently destroyed by a great fire in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087|1=1916}}), these shrines were merged and enshrined together at Mishima Shrine.<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm Mishima Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html Legend: “En no Gyōja of Mount Buzan”] - Shimoda City</ref>

The Kakizaki area is known as the place where {{ill|lt=Yoshida Shōin|Q559747|WD=Q559747|1=Yoshida Shōin}} attempted to stow away at the end of the Edo period, and statues of Yoshida Shōin have been erected within the shrine grounds and on the opposite Benten Island.<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html Statue of Yoshida Shōin (Shimoda)] - Izu & Suruga Tourism Guide</ref>

=== Enshrined deity ===

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine (Shimoda)'''|三島神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Mishima Shrine''' (みしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kakizaki, {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402|1=Shimoda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 Mishima Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> Taking its name from the place-name, it is also called '''Kakizaki Shrine''' (かきさきじんじゃ).<ref>For example, the bus stop “Kakizaki Shrine-mae,” etc.</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined on the coastal area of the Kakizaki district, east of Shimoda City, opposite Benten Island.

The date of its founding is unknown.

In the past, on the western side of the same Kakizaki district—on the southern end of Nesugata-yama, on the southern mid-slope of Buzan—there stood the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} '''Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine''' (たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ), as well as the ancient shrine '''Buzan Gongen (Takemine Shrine)''', which is identified with the deity “Jushii-jō Tatsufutawake no Myōjin” listed in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu Province Shinkaichō}}'. However, after the shrine buildings collapsed due to a violent storm in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819|1=1884}}), and were subsequently destroyed by a great fire in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087|1=1916}}), these shrines were merged and enshrined together at Mishima Shrine.<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm Mishima Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html Legend: “En no Gyōja of Mount Buzan”] - Shimoda City</ref>

The Kakizaki area is known as the place where {{ill|lt=Yoshida Shōin|Q559747|WD=Q559747|1=Yoshida Shōin}} attempted to stow away at the end of the Edo period, and statues of Yoshida Shōin have been erected within the shrine grounds and on the opposite Benten Island.<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html Statue of Yoshida Shōin (Shimoda)] - Izu & Suruga Tourism Guide</ref>

'''Mishima Shrine''' (みしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kakizaki, {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402|1=Shimoda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 Mishima Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> Taking its name from the place-name, it is also called '''Kakizaki Shrine''' (かきさきじんじゃ).<ref>For example, the bus stop “Kakizaki Shrine-mae,” etc.</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined on the coastal area of the Kakizaki district, east of Shimoda City, opposite Benten Island.

The date of its founding is unknown.

In the past, on the western side of the same Kakizaki district—on the southern end of Nesugata-yama, on the southern mid-slope of Buzan—there stood the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} '''Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine''' (たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ), as well as the ancient shrine '''Buzan Gongen (Takemine Shrine)''', which is identified with the deity “Jushii-jō Tatsufutawake no Myōjin” listed in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu Province Shinkaichō}}'. However, after the shrine buildings collapsed due to a violent storm in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819|1=1884}}), and were subsequently destroyed by a great fire in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087|1=1916}}), these shrines were merged and enshrined together at Mishima Shrine.<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm Mishima Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html Legend: “En no Gyōja of Mount Buzan”] - Shimoda City</ref>

The Kakizaki area is known as the place where {{ill|lt=Yoshida Shōin|Q559747|WD=Q559747|1=Yoshida Shōin}} attempted to stow away at the end of the Edo period, and statues of Yoshida Shōin have been erected within the shrine grounds and on the opposite Benten Island.<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html Statue of Yoshida Shōin (Shimoda)] - Izu & Suruga Tourism Guide</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine (Shimoda)'''|三島神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Mishima Shrine''' (みしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kakizaki, {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402|1=Shimoda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 Mishima Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> Taking its name from the place-name, it is also called '''Kakizaki Shrine''' (かきさきじんじゃ).<ref>For example, the bus stop “Kakizaki Shrine-mae,” etc.</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined on the coastal area of the Kakizaki district, east of Shimoda City, opposite Benten Island.

The date of its founding is unknown.

In the past, on the western side of the same Kakizaki district—on the southern end of Nesugata-yama, on the southern mid-slope of Buzan—there stood the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} '''Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine''' (たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ), as well as the ancient shrine '''Buzan Gongen (Takemine Shrine)''', which is identified with the deity “Jushii-jō Tatsufutawake no Myōjin” listed in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu Province Shinkaichō}}'. However, after the shrine buildings collapsed due to a violent storm in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819|1=1884}}), and were subsequently destroyed by a great fire in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087|1=1916}}), these shrines were merged and enshrined together at Mishima Shrine.<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm Mishima Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html Legend: “En no Gyōja of Mount Buzan”] - Shimoda City</ref>

The Kakizaki area is known as the place where {{ill|lt=Yoshida Shōin|Q559747|WD=Q559747|1=Yoshida Shōin}} attempted to stow away at the end of the Edo period, and statues of Yoshida Shōin have been erected within the shrine grounds and on the opposite Benten Island.<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html Statue of Yoshida Shōin (Shimoda)] - Izu & Suruga Tourism Guide</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine (Shimoda)'''|三島神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Mishima Shrine''' (みしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kakizaki, {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402|1=Shimoda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 Mishima Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> Taking its name from the place-name, it is also called '''Kakizaki Shrine''' (かきさきじんじゃ).<ref>For example, the bus stop “Kakizaki Shrine-mae,” etc.</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined on the coastal area of the Kakizaki district, east of Shimoda City, opposite Benten Island.

The date of its founding is unknown.

In the past, on the western side of the same Kakizaki district—on the southern end of Nesugata-yama, on the southern mid-slope of Buzan—there stood the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} '''Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine''' (たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ), as well as the ancient shrine '''Buzan Gongen (Takemine Shrine)''', which is identified with the deity “Jushii-jō Tatsufutawake no Myōjin” listed in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu Province Shinkaichō}}'. However, after the shrine buildings collapsed due to a violent storm in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819|1=1884}}), and were subsequently destroyed by a great fire in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087|1=1916}}), these shrines were merged and enshrined together at Mishima Shrine.<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm Mishima Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html Legend: “En no Gyōja of Mount Buzan”] - Shimoda City</ref>

The Kakizaki area is known as the place where {{ill|lt=Yoshida Shōin|Q559747|WD=Q559747|1=Yoshida Shōin}} attempted to stow away at the end of the Edo period, and statues of Yoshida Shōin have been erected within the shrine grounds and on the opposite Benten Island.<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html Statue of Yoshida Shōin (Shimoda)] - Izu & Suruga Tourism Guide</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine (Shimoda)'''|三島神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Mishima Shrine''' (みしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kakizaki, {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402|1=Shimoda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 Mishima Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> Taking its name from the place-name, it is also called '''Kakizaki Shrine''' (かきさきじんじゃ).<ref>For example, the bus stop “Kakizaki Shrine-mae,” etc.</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined on the coastal area of the Kakizaki district, east of Shimoda City, opposite Benten Island.

The date of its founding is unknown.

In the past, on the western side of the same Kakizaki district—on the southern end of Nesugata-yama, on the southern mid-slope of Buzan—there stood the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} '''Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine''' (たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ), as well as the ancient shrine '''Buzan Gongen (Takemine Shrine)''', which is identified with the deity “Jushii-jō Tatsufutawake no Myōjin” listed in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu Province Shinkaichō}}'. However, after the shrine buildings collapsed due to a violent storm in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819|1=1884}}), and were subsequently destroyed by a great fire in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087|1=1916}}), these shrines were merged and enshrined together at Mishima Shrine.<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm Mishima Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html Legend: “En no Gyōja of Mount Buzan”] - Shimoda City</ref>

The Kakizaki area is known as the place where {{ill|lt=Yoshida Shōin|Q559747|WD=Q559747|1=Yoshida Shōin}} attempted to stow away at the end of the Edo period, and statues of Yoshida Shōin have been erected within the shrine grounds and on the opposite Benten Island.<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html Statue of Yoshida Shōin (Shimoda)] - Izu & Suruga Tourism Guide</ref>

'''Mishima Shrine''' (みしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kakizaki, {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402|1=Shimoda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 Mishima Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> Taking its name from the place-name, it is also called '''Kakizaki Shrine''' (かきさきじんじゃ).<ref>For example, the bus stop “Kakizaki Shrine-mae,” etc.</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined on the coastal area of the Kakizaki district, east of Shimoda City, opposite Benten Island.

The date of its founding is unknown.

In the past, on the western side of the same Kakizaki district—on the southern end of Nesugata-yama, on the southern mid-slope of Buzan—there stood the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} '''Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine''' (たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ), as well as the ancient shrine '''Buzan Gongen (Takemine Shrine)''', which is identified with the deity “Jushii-jō Tatsufutawake no Myōjin” listed in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu Province Shinkaichō}}'. However, after the shrine buildings collapsed due to a violent storm in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819|1=1884}}), and were subsequently destroyed by a great fire in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087|1=1916}}), these shrines were merged and enshrined together at Mishima Shrine.<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm Mishima Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html Legend: “En no Gyōja of Mount Buzan”] - Shimoda City</ref>

The Kakizaki area is known as the place where {{ill|lt=Yoshida Shōin|Q559747|WD=Q559747|1=Yoshida Shōin}} attempted to stow away at the end of the Edo period, and statues of Yoshida Shōin have been erected within the shrine grounds and on the opposite Benten Island.<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html Statue of Yoshida Shōin (Shimoda)] - Izu & Suruga Tourism Guide</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine (Shimoda)'''|三島神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Mishima Shrine''' (みしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kakizaki, {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402|1=Shimoda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 Mishima Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> Taking its name from the place-name, it is also called '''Kakizaki Shrine''' (かきさきじんじゃ).<ref>For example, the bus stop “Kakizaki Shrine-mae,” etc.</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined on the coastal area of the Kakizaki district, east of Shimoda City, opposite Benten Island.

The date of its founding is unknown.

In the past, on the western side of the same Kakizaki district—on the southern end of Nesugata-yama, on the southern mid-slope of Buzan—there stood the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} '''Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine''' (たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ), as well as the ancient shrine '''Buzan Gongen (Takemine Shrine)''', which is identified with the deity “Jushii-jō Tatsufutawake no Myōjin” listed in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu Province Shinkaichō}}'. However, after the shrine buildings collapsed due to a violent storm in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819|1=1884}}), and were subsequently destroyed by a great fire in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087|1=1916}}), these shrines were merged and enshrined together at Mishima Shrine.<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm Mishima Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html Legend: “En no Gyōja of Mount Buzan”] - Shimoda City</ref>

The Kakizaki area is known as the place where {{ill|lt=Yoshida Shōin|Q559747|WD=Q559747|1=Yoshida Shōin}} attempted to stow away at the end of the Edo period, and statues of Yoshida Shōin have been erected within the shrine grounds and on the opposite Benten Island.<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html Statue of Yoshida Shōin (Shimoda)] - Izu & Suruga Tourism Guide</ref>

'''Mishima Shrine''' (みしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kakizaki, {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|qid=Q653402|1=Shimoda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 Mishima Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> Taking its name from the place-name, it is also called '''Kakizaki Shrine''' (かきさきじんじゃ).<ref>For example, the bus stop “Kakizaki Shrine-mae,” etc.</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined on the coastal area of the Kakizaki district, east of Shimoda City, opposite Benten Island.

The date of its founding is unknown.

In the past, on the western side of the same Kakizaki district—on the southern end of Nesugata-yama, on the southern mid-slope of Buzan—there stood the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} '''Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine''' (たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ), as well as the ancient shrine '''Buzan Gongen (Takemine Shrine)''', which is identified with the deity “Jushii-jō Tatsufutawake no Myōjin” listed in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shinkaichō|Q17193087|qid=Q17193087|1=Izu Province Shinkaichō}}'. However, after the shrine buildings collapsed due to a violent storm in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|qid=Q7819|1=1884}}), and were subsequently destroyed by a great fire in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|qid=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|qid=Q2087|1=1916}}), these shrines were merged and enshrined together at Mishima Shrine.<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm Mishima Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html Legend: “En no Gyōja of Mount Buzan”] - Shimoda City</ref>

The Kakizaki area is known as the place where {{ill|lt=Yoshida Shōin|Q559747|qid=Q559747|1=Yoshida Shōin}} attempted to stow away at the end of the Edo period, and statues of Yoshida Shōin have been erected within the shrine grounds and on the opposite Benten Island.<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html Statue of Yoshida Shōin (Shimoda)] - Izu & Suruga Tourism Guide</ref>

'''Mishima Shrine''' (みしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kakizaki, {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|qid=Q653402|1=Shimoda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401086 Mishima Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref> Taking its name from the place-name, it is also called '''Kakizaki Shrine''' (かきさきじんじゃ).<ref>For example, the bus stop “Kakizaki Shrine-mae,” etc.</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined on the coastal area of the Kakizaki district, east of Shimoda City, opposite Benten Island.

The date of its founding is unknown.

In the past, on the western side of the same Kakizaki district—on the southern end of Nesugata-yama, on the southern mid-slope of Buzan—there stood the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} '''Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine''' (たけほこつくわけのみことじんじゃ), as well as the ancient shrine '''Buzan Gongen (Takemine Shrine)''', which is identified with the deity “Jushii-jō Tatsufutawake no Myōjin” listed in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shinkaichō|Q17193087|qid=Q17193087|1=Izu Province Shinkaichō}}'. However, after the shrine buildings collapsed due to a violent storm in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|qid=Q7819|1=1884}}), and were subsequently destroyed by a great fire in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|qid=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|qid=Q2087|1=1916}}), these shrines were merged and enshrined together at Mishima Shrine.<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/misima7_title.htm Mishima Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140127-01.html Takehokotsukuwakenomikoto Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="city-buzan">[https://www.city.shimoda.shizuoka.jp/category/050201densetsu/111264.html Legend: “En no Gyōja of Mount Buzan”] - Shimoda City</ref>

The Kakizaki area is known as the place where {{ill|lt=Yoshida Shōin|Q559747|qid=Q559747|1=Yoshida Shōin}} attempted to stow away at the end of the Edo period, and statues of Yoshida Shōin have been erected within the shrine grounds and on the opposite Benten Island.<ref>[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/566.html Statue of Yoshida Shōin (Shimoda)] - Izu & Suruga Tourism Guide</ref>
